article_title: European Commission Launches TraceMap, an AI Platform to Detect Food Fraud and Speed Outbreak Response Across the EU

content_preview.title: European Commission Launches TraceMap, an AI Platform to Detect Food Fraud and Speed Outbreak Response Across the EU
content_preview.summary: The EU deploys an AI-powered traceability tool that connects national food safety databases to accelerate fraud detection and product recalls across all 27 member states.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Commission has launched TraceMap, an artificial intelligence platform designed to help national authorities detect food fraud, trace contaminated products, and accelerate recalls across all EU member states. The tool, which went live on March 10, 2026, consolidates data from multiple existing EU food safety databases and applies AI analysis to identify suspicious patterns in agri-food supply chains, according to [Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/health/2026/03/10/the-...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured food safety article with clear explanation of TraceMap's capabilities and regulatory context.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched and verified. Euronews confirms March 10 launch, Varhelyi quote verbatim, RASFF 12%/5250, 6558 outbreaks/14.5%, disease stats. EC official page confirms TRACES/RASFF/ACN integration, AI-powered traceability. Food Safety Magazine confirms infant formula pilot (cereulide/ARA oil from China), network graph visualization, core capabilities.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across all 3 sources. Statistics match precisely.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately notes unknowns about AI model details and member state capacity.
editor_notes.originality: No prior TraceMap or EU food safety AI coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid food safety/tech article with verified claims from official EU source plus two independent outlets. Approved.
